fields.summary: Integration test files being included in code coverage metrics despite exclusion config
fields.description: We have integration tests under src/it/java that we want to exclude from the SonarQube code coverage calculation because they inflate our numbers artificially. We added sonar.exclusions=**/it/java/** to our configuration but the integration test files are still appearing in the coverage report. Our quality gate is set at 80% unit test coverage and the integration tests are pushing us above this threshold making the gate meaningless.

content.text: I also tried sonar.test.exclusions=**/it/java/** but neither property seems to work. The integration test classes are still showing up in the analysis.

Deepak, there are two separate properties here that are often confused. sonar.exclusions excludes files from the analysis entirely — no issues will be raised on them. sonar.coverage.exclusions excludes files specifically from coverage calculation while still analysing them for bugs and code smells. You need to use sonar.coverage.exclusions=**/it/java/** for your use case. Also make sure the path pattern matches your actual directory structure.

sonar.coverage.exclusions did the trick. Unit test coverage is now reported at 76% which accurately reflects just our unit tests. Quality gate is working as intended now. The distinction between sonar.exclusions and sonar.coverage.exclusions is not obvious from the documentation.
